title: Fix: List container with volume filter
body:
Modify the condition in line 149 in order to list container by mounting point.


Closes #16019

Signed-off-by: SamirPS <samir.akarioh@canonical.com>

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

This PR only permit to fix the issue #16019 

 ```release-note 
Fix: List container with volume filter
 ```

comments.body: /approve
Should a test be added?
@Luap99  Should a test be added?
@SamirPS Please add a test.
@rhatdan Please tell me to what file I should add the test.
I would look at test/system/160-volumes.bats.
@rhatdan @mheon The test added is good or not?

LGTM
one of the tests was not happy, I've restarted as I suspect it to be a flake.
Okay, thanks for the information waiting for the merge then.

/lgtm
/hold
@rhatdan to know, why the "/hold"?
We always hold until tests pass, you can do a SLASHhold cancel when tests pass.
even if the tcp test fail ?

@SamirPS LGTM (Looks Good To Me) but please squash your commits.

Re: slash-lgtm and slash-hold:
* slash-lgtm tells the CI system "this is OK to merge". But:
* the CI system is not smart enough to realize "I should wait until tests pass". So it can start auto-merging even while CI is running, which is Very Bad. So:
* slash-hold, in combination with slash-lgtm, is a project admin's way of saying "this looks good to me, but we need to tell the CI system to hold its horses. Once CI passes, you (the PR owner) can type slash-hold cancel to release the hold. Please don't type that unless CI is fully green. We're trusting you."

HTH.
Oops i thinks i have wrongly done my squash

> Oops i thinks i have wrongly done my squash

Yes, there's still a merge commit.
Yeah but when i squash this commit I have modified file :45

> Yeah but when i squash this commit I have modified file :45

Try `git pull --rebase $REMOTE/main`.  That should get rid of the merge commit.
@vrothberg Thanks for the command, it's good now?
Thanks you for the command! waiting for the test now

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *mheon*, *SamirPS* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@vrothberg @edsantiago @rhatdan Can I remove the SLASHhold or not ? I because the int remote fedora-27 failed because of the network test.
DO NOT REMOVE IT.

I'm sorry I wasn't clear. This is the rule: you can remove the hold only when tests go green. You cannot ever ever remove it otherwise.

The failure is a flake, it is rerunning. Once it goes green, I or someone else will remove the hold. Since it seems I can't explain properly, and there's a possibility of confusion, please do not touch anything for now. Thank you.
Okay sorry, i understand now

No problem. It's complicated and unintuitive. If you look at CI right now or in the next few minutes, you'll see that the flaking test has passed, but there are still a bunch more tests running. That's because CI is a cascade:  certain tests will only run if their precursors pass. Here's a [visualization](https://github.com/containers/podman/wiki/Cirrus-Dependency-Visualization). So now we still have another hour or so to wait to see if CI goes green.

Anyhow, the critical thing to remember is: CI must be green in order to merge.
All green right now

Indeed!

/hold cancel

Thank you @SamirPS !
